evals.prompt: Write a Pest feature test in Laravel

evals.expected_output: Add uses(RefreshDatabase::class) in Pest or use RefreshDatabase trait in PHPUnit TestCase; database rolled back between each test; use DatabaseTransactions for faster but non-truncating isolation

evals.prompt: Create Eloquent Factories for test data in Laravel
evals.expected_output: php artisan make:factory PostFactory --model=Post; define definition(): array with fake() data; use Post::factory()->count(3)->create(['user_id' => $user->id]) in tests; never DB::table()->insert()

evals.prompt: Mock external services in Laravel tests
evals.expected_output: $this->mock(PaymentService::class)->shouldReceive('charge')->once()->with(100)->andReturn(true); or use $this->spy() for loose verification; never make real network calls in tests

evals.prompt: Assert HTTP responses in Laravel feature tests
Set up in-memory SQLite for Laravel unit tests
evals.expected_output: Set <env name='DB_CONNECTION' value='sqlite'/> and <env name='DB_DATABASE' value=':memory:'/> in phpunit.xml; fast but SQLite lacks some MySQL features; use for unit tests, real DB for feature tests

should_not_trigger: Write Jest tests for a React component
PHPUnit tests for a Symfony application
